title: HTTP server layout cribs burrow's server module
rationale: Operator can flip between the two codebases without retraining: same Route/RouteContext/ServeHandle surface, same auth seam, same error envelope. The thin glue style (handlers wrap runs/registry/projects modules 1:1) keeps the wire layer small enough to read end-to-end.

content: Static UI catch-all registers AFTER API routes; the UI handler explicitly refuses API prefixes (/agents, /projects, /runs, /healthz, /readyz) so an API path with a typo returns a 404 envelope instead of accidentally rendering the SPA shell. Other GETs fall through to index.html so React Router handles deep links.

name: BridgeRegistry per-server lifecycle
description: Per-server registry owns live bridgeRunStream controllers. POST /runs registers a bridge via deps.bridges.start(); bootBridges resumes bridges for in-flight (queued|running) runs with burrow_run_id at startup; handle.stop() aborts everyone via stopAll() and awaits their drain. Resolved bridges auto-detach in their .finally so the registry stays small. Tests inject a stub bridge factory to avoid a real burrow.

title: WarrenError status mapping table; BurrowError passes through
rationale: Each WarrenError subclass maps to a fixed status in renderError: NotFoundError=404, ValidationError=400, StateTransitionError=409, BurrowUnreachableError=503, CanopyUnavailableError=503, ProjectUnavailableError=503, AgentSchemaError=422, RunSpawnError=500. BurrowError (rehydrated from burrow's HTTP envelope) forwards with its code+hint preserved so the same {code,message,hint} consumer works against warren and burrow without a second decoder.

content: Warren server env vars are warren-namespaced: WARREN_API_TOKEN (bearer; required unless --no-auth), WARREN_BIND_HOST/WARREN_BIND_PORT (TCP, default 0.0.0.0:8080), WARREN_BIND_SOCKET (unix; wins over host/port), WARREN_DATA_DIR (default /data), WARREN_DB_PATH (default <DATA_DIR>/warren.db), WARREN_UI_DIST_DIR (default <cwd>/src/ui/dist), WARREN_DISABLE_UI=1|true (no static serving), WARREN_LOG_LEVEL (pino level, default info).

registry/projects SpawnFn shape is (cmd: readonly string[], opts: { cwd: string; timeoutMs?: number }) => Promise<{ stdout: string; stderr: string; exitCode: number }> — non-zero exit codes are part of the result, not an exception. The default impl in handlers.ts uses Bun.spawn (with a setTimeout-based kill on timeoutMs) rather than execFile because execFile throws on non-zero, which doesn't match the SpawnResult contract.

name: ServerDeps wiring for tests
description: ServerDeps bundles repos + burrowClient + broker + bridges + canopyConfig + projectsConfig + logger + uiDistDir + optional now(). startServer takes ServerDeps so tests can spin up the wire layer without a real burrow socket: pass a stub fetch into BurrowClient, a stub bridge factory into createBridgeRegistry, and uiDistDir=null to skip static serving. main.ts owns the prod wiring; tests assemble deps inline.

title: POST /runs registers a bridge synchronously after spawnRun
rationale: Without deps.bridges.start(result.run.id, result.burrowRun.id) inside the createRun handler, the dispatched run emits events into burrow but warren's bridge never subscribes, so /runs/:id/events streams nothing. The bridge MUST be started in the same turn as the spawnRun success — there is no other surface where this happens. boot-time recoverActiveRunStreams covers the post-restart case, but never the post-spawn case.

title: Shared src/diagnostics/checks.ts backs /readyz and warren doctor
rationale: Phase 13 (warren-a29f) needed both /readyz and warren doctor to probe burrow + canopy clone existence + canopy clone cleanliness + bwrap availability. Duplicating the checks across the HTTP handler and the CLI command would have let the two surfaces drift. checks.ts exports pure functions (checkBwrap, checkCanopyClone, checkCanopyClean, checkBurrowReachable) returning {name, ok, message?, hint?} that doctor renders as NDJSON and /readyz packs into a single envelope. New readiness probes should land here, not in handlers.ts or doctor.ts.

content: ServerDeps.spawn is the spawn seam /readyz uses for the Phase-13 bwrap + canopy_clean probes. main.ts wires the production Bun.spawn adaptor (a third copy of defaultSpawn alongside handlers.ts and cli/output.ts; deliberate per existing layering — neither surface imports the other). Tests pass an okSpawn stub that returns exit 0 for bwrap --version and empty stdout for git status --porcelain.

Auth gate exempts /healthz plus every non-API path. /healthz is exempt because liveness probes can't carry a token; non-API paths (SPA shell /, /index.html, /assets/*, React Router deep links) are exempt so a fresh browser can reach Login.tsx to enter the bearer token. /readyz and the rest of the API stay gated. The predicate is isAuthExempt(pathname) in handlers.ts; API surface determined via the shared API_PREFIXES list (/agents, /projects, /runs, /healthz, /readyz).

description: Initial auth posture used AUTH_EXEMPT_PATHS = new Set(['/healthz']) — locking the SPA out of its own login page. Hitting GET / returned 401, but Login.tsx is the only place a user enters the bearer token. Result: warren UI was unreachable from a fresh browser.
resolution: Replace the Set with isAuthExempt(pathname) predicate that exempts /healthz plus every non-API path. Test fixture in server.test.ts spins up a real Bun.serve with a temp dist dir and asserts /, /assets/app.js, and SPA deep links return 200 while /agents and /readyz still 401.

title: API_PREFIXES owned by handlers.ts, imported by ui.ts and isAuthExempt
rationale: ui.ts originally hardcoded its own ['/agents','/projects','/runs','/healthz','/readyz'] for SPA-fallback rejection. Adding the auth predicate would have created a third copy. Single source in handlers.ts (next to ROUTE_TABLE) keeps the API surface in lockstep across the SPA fallback and the auth gate; one list to update when a new top-level route lands. The predicate (isApiPath/isAuthExempt) is also exported, so tests and any future call site share the same definition.

content: POST /projects 201 returns the bare ProjectRow ({id, gitUrl, localPath, defaultBranch, addedAt}) — not nested under a 'project' key. DELETE /projects/:id 200 also returns the bare row. Listing wraps: GET /projects returns {projects: [...]}.

Warren's Bun.serve passes idleTimeout: 0 by default (DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS in src/server/server.ts) so long-lived NDJSON streams (GET /runs/:id/events?follow=1) aren't killed by Bun's 10s per-request idle timer. Override via ServeOptions.idleTimeout (tests pass small values to assert wiring).

name: bridge-reconnect-on-error
description: BridgeRegistry.start() wraps bridgeRunStream in an exponential-backoff reconnect loop (1s→2s→4s→8s→16s→30s cap, DEFAULT_RECONNECT_BACKOFF_MS). Stops only on: registry abort (stopAll), bridge naturally ending (errored=false), or warren DB showing the run terminal (RunsRepo.get + TERMINAL_RUN_STATES). Each reconnect re-invokes bridgeRunStream which re-reads MAX(events.burrow_event_seq) so dedupe is automatic. Solves: burrow's per-request 10s idleTimeout closing /runs/:id/stream mid-run, leaving warren's bridge dead while the agent kept emitting events.

content: GET /projects/:id/warren-config (warren-435b) returns the LoadedWarrenConfig envelope verbatim ({triggers, defaults, errors}); 404 on unknown id, 503 mapped from WarrenConfigUnavailableError when the clone is missing; the handler prefers deps.warrenConfigs.get for cache hits and falls back to a direct loadWarrenConfig when warrenConfigs is undefined (test deps may omit it).

WarrenConfigUnavailableError joins BurrowUnreachableError/CanopyUnavailableError/ProjectUnavailableError as a 503-mapped WarrenError in src/server/errors.ts warrenStatusFor — every '<surface> unavailable' class maps to 503, code passthrough preserved.

Logger adapters per-module: bridgeLoggerFromPino + schedulerLoggerFromPino in src/server/main.ts narrow pino's Logger to the optional-method shape each downstream module accepts (bridges' optional info?/warn?/error?, scheduler's required-method TickLogger). Adapters live next to bootServer so adding a new pino-consumer is a 5-line addition, not a cross-module type juggle.

Project-scoped HTTP routes (warren-435b, warren-99c3) always require the project row first (deps.repos.projects.require(id)) before any warren-config load — keeps the 404 contract honest when both an unknown project id AND a malformed .warren/ could collide.

src/server/main.ts (warren-41a2) constructs BurrowClientPool.fromEnv({env, repos, now?}) at boot instead of BurrowClient.fromEnv(env). The legacy burrowClient variable is now derived via burrowClientPool.singleton() and passed through to bridges/scheduler/ServerDeps unchanged — full migration off the singleton lands in pl-9ba1 steps 4 (spawnRun) and 5 (fan-out reads). Shutdown closes the pool (burrowClientPool.close()) instead of the individual client; pool.close uses allSettled so a misbehaving worker can't block server stop.

ServerDeps (src/server/types.ts, warren-39c3) gained `burrowClientPool: BurrowClientPool` alongside the legacy `burrowClient: BurrowClient`. POST /runs and POST /projects/:id/triggers/:triggerId/run thread the pool into spawnRun for placement; cancel/steer/readyz still read `burrowClient` (singleton-derived) until step 5 routes them through pool.clientFor. bootServer constructs the pool once; pool.singleton() survives there as the back-compat shim feeding the un-migrated paths.

bootScheduler (src/server/scheduler.ts, warren-39c3) takes `burrowClientPool: BurrowClientPool` (not `burrowClient`) and threads it into spawnRunFn so scheduled cron + scheduled-for dispatches resolve placement through the pool the same way POST /runs does. The legacy burrowClient parameter is gone from the scheduler surface.

GET /burrows (src/server/handlers.ts listBurrowsHandler, warren-14ad) fans out http.burrows.list across the BurrowClientPool via fanOutAcrossWorkers, unions the rows, and sorts by createdAt ascending. Wire envelope is { burrows: Burrow[], workerErrors: { worker, message }[] } — partial-failure surfaces the dropped workers rather than failing the response. Accepts ?kind / ?state / ?projectRoot; kind/state are validated against satisfies-bound whitelists in BURROW_KIND_VALUES / BURROW_STATE_VALUES so adding an upstream value is silent (a route that doesn't filter on it still works), while a value that is not a BurrowKind/BurrowState fails tsc.

GET /burrows/:id (src/server/handlers.ts getBurrowHandler, warren-14ad) is sticky-by-burrow: the handler first checks deps.repos.burrows.get(id) and throws NotFoundError (404) if warren has no placement record, otherwise resolves the worker via pool.clientFor({burrowId}) and forwards the call. The 404 short-circuit is required because placeForBurrow raises NoEligibleWorkerError for missing rows (same class used for the no-healthy-workers case, mapped to 503 in src/server/errors.ts) — without the explicit pre-check, missing-burrow would surface as 503 instead of 404.

NoEligibleWorkerError and StickyWorkerUnreachableError both map to HTTP 503 in src/server/errors.ts warrenStatusFor (warren-14ad). The 'burrow has no placement record' case (placeForBurrow → NoEligibleWorkerError) needs an explicit NotFoundError pre-check in the handler if 404 semantics are desired — same error class is used for the no-healthy-workers case where 503 is correct.

name: Worker probe loop
description: src/server/probe.ts owns periodic worker state reconciliation (warren-0f0c). Same shape as triggers/tick.ts: single-flight inFlight promise, disabled flag for unconditional bootServer wiring, runOnce test seam, NOOP_HANDLE symbol for disabled mode. State transitions: probe ok flips unreachable -> healthy, probe failed flips healthy -> unreachable. Draining is sticky — operator-set state is never overwritten by probe; the only exit from draining is POST /workers/:name/drain {drain: false}. Pool entries missing a workers row log worker_probe.missing_row and skip. Boot wires the probe into the stop-chain between scheduler and bridges.

content: POST /workers/:name/drain (src/server/handlers.ts) drains via burrow-then-warren ordering: first POST /admin/drain through pool.get(name).setDrain(body.drain) wrapped in withTransportMapping, then on success workers.setState(name, drain ? draining : healthy). If burrow call fails (404 not_found on older burrows, ECONNREFUSED, auth mismatch) the warren row stays put so operators retry rather than drifting from burrow's actual bit. Body defaults to {drain: true} when empty — common operator path — but accepts {drain: false} to un-drain.

GET /workers (src/server/handlers.ts) returns the workers table joined with pool.names() so each row carries a registered:boolean flag. Surfaces drift between [workers] config materializing rows in SQLite and the in-memory BurrowClientPool's client map — a registered=false row is the canonical signal a config sync missed an entry. Empty table returns {workers: []}.

bootServer (src/server/main.ts) picks BurrowClientPool factory based on loadWarrenServerConfigFromFile().workers.length: >0 → fromConfig (+ requireSharedBurrowToken), 0 → fromEnv (zero-config back-compat). pool.singleton() back-compat shim still consumes the legacy burrowClient variable for bridges/steer/cancel/boot probe — single-worker [workers] configs work today; multi-worker boot throws at singleton() until those consumers migrate to clientFor.

name: two-worker integration harness
description: src/server/integration.multi-worker.test.ts (warren-a801 / pl-9ba1 step 10) boots a real warren HTTP server in front of a BurrowClientPool containing two stubbed burrow workers and drives end-to-end scenarios through HTTP. Each stub worker keeps an in-memory burrow registry so fan-out reads have non-trivial state to union, accepts the shared bearer token from BurrowClientPool.fromConfig on every call, and exposes a 'killed' boolean to simulate worker unreachability mid-list. The harness shares a single monotonic clock closure across both stub workers so createdAt timestamps reflect global call order rather than per-worker counter ties — without this the createdAt-asc sort assertion in fan-out tests breaks.

content: Test files that boot startServer should reuse jsonResponse from src/server/response.ts rather than inline a local Response wrapper — five test files (handlers.test.ts, handlers.burrows.test.ts, handlers.workers.test.ts, spawn.test.ts, integration.multi-worker.test.ts) had historically duplicated it; integration.multi-worker.test.ts is the first to import the production one.

BridgeRegistry.start(runId, burrowRunId, burrowId) (src/server/types.ts + bridges.ts, warren-c0c9) gained a third arg: every caller now threads the burrowId so each bridge can resolve its owning worker via pool.clientFor. Production callers: handlers.ts POST /runs + the trigger Run-Now path (deps.bridges.start(result.run.id, result.burrowRun.id, result.burrow.id)), scheduler.ts (input.bridges.start), and bootBridges (registry.start(run.id, run.burrowRunId, run.burrowId)).

description: bootBridges crashes the supervisor with an unhandled rejection when an in-flight run's burrow_id has no row in the burrows placement table (legacy runs predating pl-9ba1)
resolution: Immediate unblock: stop container, mark orphan runs state=failed,failure_reason=crashed in /data/warren.db (mount warren_warren_data into a one-shot --entrypoint bun container, run UPDATE against runs WHERE state IN ('queued','running')), restart. Code fix lives at src/server/bridges.ts:107 (.catch on done) and src/server/bridges.ts:289-326 (pre-flight repos.burrows.get before registry.start, mirror the no_burrow_id skip pattern). NoEligibleWorkerError originates at src/runs/placement.ts:96 and escapes through runWithReconnect's fire-and-forget done promise.

name: bridge-fire-and-forget-needs-catch
description: createBridgeRegistry.start (src/server/bridges.ts) fires `runWithReconnect` as an un-awaited promise and registers a `.finally` only. Any synchronous-in-bridge throw — e.g. pool.clientFor() raising NoEligibleWorkerError on a pre-pl-9ba1 orphan burrow_id — rejects an unhandled promise and Bun terminates the process, which crash-loops the supervisor under docker `restart: unless-stopped`. The fix (warren-018a) chains `.catch` BEFORE `.finally`: log the error and write a synthetic kind='bridge_fatal', stream='system' event to repos.events so the UI shows why the bridge stopped. The run row stays in its current state for the reaper to finalize.

content: bootBridges (src/server/bridges.ts) pre-flights three layers before registry.start: (1) burrowRunId !== null, (2) burrowId !== null, (3) repos.burrows.get(burrowId) !== null. Layer 3 (warren-018a) catches pre-pl-9ba1 orphan runs whose burrow_id has no `burrows` row — surfacing them in result.skipped with reason='no_placement' instead of crashing the supervisor when bridgeRunStream's first pool.clientFor() raises NoEligibleWorkerError.

ServerDeps (src/server/types.ts) gained an optional 'db?: AnyWarrenDb' field for the /readyz db_reachable probe (R-13 pl-f17e step 5, warren-e2ea). bootServer always wires it from openDatabase; tests may omit and checkDatabaseReachable degrades to ok=true. /readyz emits db_reachable as the FIRST check (ahead of burrow_reachable, agents, canopy_*, bwrap, warren_config) so a failed db pin masks downstream cascading failures and surfaces the root cause first.

Bridge registry (src/server/bridges.ts createBridgeRegistry) accepts warrenConfigs + portAllocator + previewLaunchConfig (warren-f156). The terminal-detect inline-reap path loads the project's .warren/defaults.json preview block via resolveProjectPreviewConfig and forwards a resolved ServerPreviewConfig to reap when outcome=succeeded. type='static' previews are filtered out here (follow-up seed). bootServer (src/server/main.ts) wires the singleton allocator from db.dialect==='sqlite' (postgres deferred to pl-f17e follow-up) + loadPreviewLaunchConfigFromEnv(env) for WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST.

GET /readyz (src/server/handlers.ts, warren-ea6b) preview_max_live check uses createRunPreviewsRepo(deps.db).countActivePreviews() wrapped via { count: () => previews.countActivePreviews() } so the diagnostic surface stays narrow (PreviewLiveCountProbe). Falls back to ok:true with informational message when deps.db is undefined or dialect!=='sqlite'. ServerDeps gained previewMaxLive:number, wired from WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIVE at bootServer.

Preview proxy preamble (src/preview/proxy.ts, warren-8a10 / SPEC §11.L) runs in src/server/server.ts handleRequest BEFORE the auth gate and route match. Wired via ServeOptions.previewProxy from bootServer; previews use signed-cookie auth keyed off Host, not the bearer header the API gate inspects. createPreviewProxyHandler returns null when Host does not match run-X.previewHost and the normal pipeline takes over.

isAuthExempt (src/server/handlers.ts) whitelists GET /runs/:id/preview/login via PREVIEW_LOGIN_PATH_RE (warren-8a10). The browser arrives without an Authorization header and validates the bearer via ?token=<WARREN_API_TOKEN>; the handler does its own constant-time compare via PreviewAuth.verifyLoginToken. All other /runs/* surfaces stay gated.

createPreviewAuth (src/preview/cookie.ts, warren-8a10) derives the HMAC-SHA256 cookie key from WARREN_API_TOKEN via SHA-256(token || "::preview-cookie-v1"). The same PreviewAuth instance powers verifyLoginToken (constant-time bearer compare) and signCookie/verifyCookie (per-runId payload v1.<runId>.<expiresMs>.<sigB64>). Two surfaces, one secret — fresh-install operators don preview have a second token to manage.

POST /runs/:id/preview/teardown (warren-d725) handler in src/server/handlers.ts narrows deps.db on dialect inline (the same pattern previewMaxLiveReadyzCheck and previewPortAllocatorReadyzCheck use) and returns 503 {error.code:'preview_teardown_unavailable'} when deps.db is undefined OR not sqlite. The handler constructs createRunPreviewsRepo(deps.db) per-request — trivial allocation cost vs. threading another optional repo through ServerDeps. teardownLoggerFor adapts deps.logger (pino-shaped) onto the Record<string,unknown> signature the teardown/eviction modules want.

title: Preview auth uses a signed cookie keyed off WARREN_API_TOKEN, not bearer / OAuth / basic-auth
rationale: ROADMAP R-19 listed four auth options (a) signed-cookie from a login endpoint, (b) GitHub OAuth, (c) per-run basic-auth password in reap summary, (d) no-auth. SPEC §11.L locked (a). Bearer-in-header is impossible for a browser hitting run-<id>.<host> directly. Basic-auth UX is hostile (every link click prompts) and operators have to scrape the reap event log to find the password. GitHub OAuth requires R-18 (GitHub App) and adds an installation dance for every operator. (a) reuses the bearer token warren already trusts: createPreviewAuth derives the HMAC-SHA256 cookie key from WARREN_API_TOKEN via SHA-256(token || '::preview-cookie-v1') — one secret, two surfaces. Cookie scope is Domain=.<warren-host>; Path=/; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax. Login route is auth-exempt (token in ?token=); proxy preamble verifies HMAC before forwarding, 401 (not 502) on unauth. GitHub OAuth defers to R-18; (c)/(d) rejected.

content: bridges.ts resolveProjectPrTemplate (src/server/bridges.ts, warren-bd49) mirrors resolveProjectPreviewConfig: walks runs.projectId → projects → warrenConfigs.get to pull the cached .warren/pr-template.md overrides. Returns undefined when seam isn't wired, no project, no template, or empty overrides — reap then renders defaults. Loader errors fall through to undefined; operators see them via /projects/:id/warren-config.

src/server/main.ts (warren-adfb) constructs PreviewPortAllocator + startPreviewEvictionWorker UNCONDITIONALLY for both dialects — the previous 'db.dialect === sqlite ? ... : undefined' ternaries were the reason a pg-deployed warren silently skipped preview launches (reap.completed reported previewState:null with no preview events). Same for handlers.ts previewPortAllocatorReadyzCheck and previewMaxLiveReadyzCheck (deleted the dialect skip messages) and cli/commands/doctor.ts previewPortAllocatorCheck. Both modules are now dialect-polymorphic via DrizzleAdapter (mx-1d9f7a).

previewLoginHandler (src/server/handlers.ts, warren-edff) branches on deps.previewMode: subdomain mode validates redirect against https://run-<id>.<previewHost>/ (requires previewHost); path mode validates against the inbound ctx.url.origin under /p/<id>/ and accepts relative URLs (resolved against the inbound origin). The 400 'preview_redirect_invalid' hint also differs per mode. Path mode no longer requires deps.previewHost to be set.

bootServer (src/server/main.ts, warren-edff) wires previewAuth + previewProxy when serverConfig.token !== null AND (mode === 'path' OR previewHost !== null). Path mode no longer requires WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST — the proxy preamble + cookie scope both ride on the warren host itself. ServerDeps now carries previewMode (defaults to subdomain in handlers for safety; bootServer always sets it explicitly).

GET /preview/config (warren-016d) is bearer-gated and returns {mode, host}. /preview is in API_PREFIXES so isApiPath catches it; the auth-exempt PREVIEW_LOGIN_PATH_RE only matches /runs/<id>/preview/login, never /preview/config. No conflict with the path-mode proxy preamble (which intercepts /p/<run-id>/, not /preview/).

name: auth-exempt /version endpoint
description: GET /version returns {version: VERSION} and is auth-exempt so the UI sidebar (Layout.tsx) can render the running warren version on the login screen without a token. Added to API_PREFIXES and ui.ts's API-vs-SPA fallback. UI fetches via React Query with staleTime: Infinity since version is stable for the process lifetime.

content: POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh (src/server/handlers.ts refreshProjectAgentsHandler, pl-fef5 step 6 / warren-7777) loads the project via repos.projects.require (404 on unknown id) then calls refreshProjectAgents through projectCanopyClient(deps, project.localPath). Returns the decorated RefreshProjectResult shape {projectId, registered: AgentRow[] with source, skipped, removed}.

GET /agents and GET /agents/:name (src/server/handlers.ts, pl-fef5 step 6 / warren-7777) honor optional ?projectId=<id>. List delegates to agents.listAll({projectId}) → global ∪ project tier. Get switches between agents.get(name) (no filter, global only) and agents.resolve(name, {projectId}) (project-first with global fallback). Empty ?projectId= is rejected with 400 validation_error to surface typo'd queries instead of silently collapsing to global-only.

POST /agents/refresh (src/server/handlers.ts refreshAgents, pl-fef5 step 6 / warren-7777) iterates every project after the library pass. Per-project failures are caught into a projectErrors:[{projectId, code, message}] array so one bad .canopy/ does not poison the batch — successes land in projects:[{projectId, registered, skipped, removed}] alongside the library's top-level clone/registered/skipped/removed fields. The library pass still uses the module-local defaultSpawn; project-tier spawns route through deps.spawn ?? defaultSpawn (test seam mirrored by POST /projects/:id/refresh).

POST /runs/:id/preview/teardown (src/server/handlers.ts previewTeardownHandler, warren-a743) is dialect-polymorphic — works on sqlite and postgres. The only deps.db precondition is 'is wired at all' (503 preview_teardown_unavailable when undefined). The prior 'dialect === sqlite' gate from warren-d725 was vestigial: createRunPreviewsRepo (src/preview/eviction.ts, warren-adfb) is dialect-polymorphic and the eviction worker already drives the same claimTeardown CAS on pg every tick. Updates mx-cc197f, which described the old gate.

GET /runs/:id/events (src/server/handlers.ts streamRunEventsHandler, warren-a8c3) snapshots run.plot_id at stream-open time and tags every NDJSON envelope with it as the top-level plotId field. The runs row's plot_id is set at spawn and never mutates, so the closure-captured value is authoritative for the life of the stream. Consumers can route Plot-mirrored events (warren-7e0f) without a second GET /runs/:id call.

POST /plan-runs (warren-f923) handler validates project.hasSeeds before reading the plan; .seeds/-less projects get ProjectLacksSeedsError (400, code 'project_lacks_seeds'). All-children-closed plans get PlanHasNoOpenChildrenError (same shape, code 'plan_has_no_open_children'). Both errors extend WarrenError and are mapped to 400 alongside ValidationError in src/server/errors.ts warrenStatusFor — extending ValidationError doesn't work because its 'readonly code = "validation_error"' is a literal type.

GET /plan-runs/:id/events (warren-f923) tails the union of every child run's events: snapshot via events.listByRunIds(runIds), then live broker.subscribe per child runId. A 2s polling watcher picks up newly-dispatched children so a stream opened mid-plan-run picks up child N+1 without a reconnect. Read-only — never writes an event row.

POST /plan-runs/:id/cancel (warren-f923) finds the in-flight child (state in dispatched|running|pr_open) and forwards a cancelRun call with reason='plan_run_cancelled:<planRunId>'. The cancel of the underlying child run is best-effort — a failure is logged but doesn't tear down the plan-run cancel itself. Idempotent: an already-terminal plan-run returns {alreadyTerminal: true, cancelledChild: null}.

POST /plan-runs gates plot_id via a typed ProjectLacksPlotError (code=project_lacks_plot, 400) raised in src/server/handlers.ts:createPlanRunHandler right after the hasSeeds gate. Mirrors ProjectLacksSeedsError's shape but is semantically distinct from the single-run path which still uses bare ValidationError in src/runs/spawn.ts:257 — the plan-runs error surface is uniformly typed (warren-c900 / pl-7937 Phase 2 step 3). Empty-string plotId is treated as 'not supplied' to match the single-run handler's posture.

PlanRun gates stack: src/server/handlers.ts createPlanRunHandler runs the hasSeeds gate (ProjectLacksSeedsError) BEFORE the plot_id/hasPlot gate (ProjectLacksPlotError), so plot_id never short-circuits the .seeds/ requirement. A project with hasPlot=true + hasSeeds=false + plot_id still rejects as project_lacks_seeds — locked in by handlers.plan-runs.test.ts 'stacks seeds-gate ahead of plot-gate' (warren-909c / pl-7937 step 6). PlanRun-with-plot only lights up when BOTH .seeds/ AND .plot/ are present; the gates are not independent.

src/plots/ (warren-7e85 / pl-9d6a step 1) is the server-side Plot aggregation module — typed facade over UserPlotClient.query/rebuildIndex/read/events across hasPlot=true projects. createPlotAggregator({projectsRepo, logger, clientFactory?, cacheTtlMs?, now?}) returns a PlotAggregator with listSummaries({status?}) and invalidate(projectId?). Fan-out is Promise.all per-project; each per-project branch retries the index query once after a best-effort rebuildIndex() on first failure (mx-239786 pattern, same shape as src/runs/spawn.ts:defaultPlotAppender and src/plan-runs/plot-appender.ts). 5s in-memory cache keyed by project_id stores the unfiltered PlotSummary[] for that project; status filter is applied client-side post-cache so filtered + unfiltered calls share the window. invalidate() clears one entry (or all when omitted) so mutating handlers (POST /plots, POST /plots/:id/*) can force the next list fresh. Per-project failures are logged as 'plots.aggregate_project_failed' and DROPPED from the merged result — one broken .plot/ never 500s GET /plots deployment-wide. Empty-deployment contract: when zero projects have hasPlot=true, listSummaries returns the byte-identical EMPTY_PLOT_SUMMARIES frozen reference (pinned by aggregate.test.ts); the factory is NOT opened in that path. Sort is last_event_ts desc with id as the stable tiebreak. PlotSummary.last_event_ts/_actor read the events() tail and fall back to plot.updated_at + empty actor when the event log is empty. intent_goal_preview truncates intent.goal to ≤160 chars with a U+2026 ellipsis (buildIntentGoalPreview, exported).

createPlotResolver (src/plots/resolver.ts, warren-7e85 / pl-9d6a step 1) is the owning-project lookup for per-Plot handlers (GET /plots/:id, POST /plots/:id/{intent,status,attachments,questions/:event_id/answer}). resolve(plotId) returns the ProjectRow whose .plot/ index contains plotId, or null when no hasPlot=true project's index does. Implementation rides on aggregator.listSummaries() (no status filter) so it shares the same 5s per-project cache — a GET /plots that brackets a GET /plots/:id within the TTL does at most one index read per project. Returns null on empty-string plotId without consulting the aggregator (defensive against bad URL params). Also returns null when the cached summary's project_id is no longer in projectsRepo.listAll (defensive against project-delete races). V1 Plot IDs are globally unique (plot-xxxxxxxx) so the first match wins; handlers translate null → typed 404 at the response edge.

GET /plots (src/server/handlers.ts listPlotsHandler, warren-c167 / pl-9d6a step 2) reads through ServerDeps.plotAggregator (the src/plots/index.ts PlotAggregator threaded by bootServer). Optional ?status= filter validated at handler edge against @os-eco/plot-cli PLOT_STATUSES (drafting|ready|active|done|archived); unknown values raise ValidationError → 400 + code=validation_error. Empty/missing ?status= treated as no filter. Empty-deployments contract: when deps.plotAggregator is undefined or aggregator reports zero hasPlot projects, returns 200 {plots: []} (byte-identical empty body). Mutating plot handlers (later pl-9d6a steps) will call deps.plotAggregator.invalidate(projectId) after writes. Route added to API_PREFIXES in handlers.ts AND mirrored in src/preview/proxy.ts WARREN_API_PATH_PREFIXES (warren-63e1 parity test pins the duplication).

POST /plots (src/server/handlers.ts createPlotHandler, warren-194e / pl-9d6a step 3) accepts {project_id, name?, intent?, dispatcher_handle?}; name defaults to 'Untitled Plot' when omitted, explicit empty/whitespace string rejects with 400. Gated on project.hasPlot via typed ProjectLacksPlotError (mirrors mx-afe7e0's shape from POST /plan-runs — same 400 envelope + project_lacks_plot code). Dispatcher handle resolves through resolveDispatcherHandle (mx-6a9788) so malformed input downgrades to 'operator'. intent body is a partial JSON object {goal?, non_goals?, constraints?, success_criteria?}; unknown fields reject with 400 so 'goals'/'nongoals' typos surface. Delegates to deps.plotCreator seam (defaults to defaultPlotCreator in src/plots/creator.ts) which opens a UserPlotClient against <project>/.plot/, calls PlotStore.create({name}), applies the intent patch via editIntent when non-empty, then reads the fresh plot + event tail under the same client lifecycle. NOT fire-and-log: failure propagates synchronously as the HTTP response (contrasts mx-92e6b3's plan_run_dispatched posture — there the user is waiting on the PlanRun, here on the Plot itself). Successful create invalidates the plotAggregator cache entry for the owning project so the next GET /plots / PlotResolver.resolve sees the fresh row without the 5s TTL wait.

GET /plots/:id handler (src/server/handlers.ts getPlotHandler, warren-961e / pl-9d6a step 8) returns the full Plot envelope {id, name, status, intent, attachments[], event_log[], project_id}. Resolves owning project via deps.plotResolver (test seam, default createPlotResolver over plotAggregator's 5s cache); null → typed 404. Defensive hasPlot re-check on the resolved ProjectRow surfaces ProjectLacksPlotError (400 / project_lacks_plot) — same envelope as POST /plots + POST /plan-runs (mx-afe7e0). Read via deps.plotReader (defaultPlotReader src/plots/reader.ts opens UserPlotClient at <project>/.plot/, snapshots plot+events in parallel, sorts events ascending by .at). Read-only client uses fixed user:operator actor — per-Plot mutation handlers in later pl-9d6a steps thread real dispatcher_handles. No fire-and-log: reader errors propagate as 500.

ServerDeps now exposes plotResolver?: PlotResolver and plotReader?: PlotReader test seams alongside the existing plotAggregator/plotCreator (src/server/types.ts). bootServer wires defaults in src/server/main.ts: createPlotResolver({projectsRepo, aggregator: plotAggregator}) and defaultPlotReader. Tests stub at either seam to skip disk; when plotResolver is undefined GET /plots/:id returns 404 to preserve the non-Plot-deployment byte-identical contract (mirrors EMPTY_PLOT_SUMMARIES posture on GET /plots).

POST /plots/:id/intent (src/server/handlers.ts editPlotIntentHandler, warren-896f / pl-9d6a step 9) accepts the intent patch fields flat at the top level of the body — {goal?, non_goals?, constraints?, success_criteria?, dispatcher_handle?} — NOT nested under an 'intent' key like POST /plots does. parseTopLevelIntentPatch (handlers.ts) is the dedicated validator: unknown top-level fields reject with 400 so 'goals'/'nongoals' typos surface; dispatcher_handle is allow-listed (read separately by the handler). Resolves the owning project via deps.plotResolver (same cache-backed path as GET /plots/:id, mx-0ef603), runs the defensive hasPlot re-check (ProjectLacksPlotError → 400), then delegates to deps.plotIntentEditor (default defaultPlotIntentEditor in src/plots/intent-editor.ts). The editor opens a UserPlotClient, reads the Plot to enforce SPEC §6's frozen-at-done rule via assertIntentMutable (status === 'done' || 'archived' → PlotIntentFrozenError → 409 / plot_intent_frozen, mapped in src/server/errors.ts alongside StateTransitionError), applies the patch via PlotHandle.editIntent, then snapshots plot+events into the full envelope. NOT fire-and-log: failures (including PlotIntentFrozenError) propagate synchronously as the HTTP response — contrasts mx-92e6b3's plan_run_dispatched posture where the user is waiting on the PlanRun not the Plot mirror. Aggregator cache for the owning project is invalidated post-edit so the next GET /plots sees the new intent_goal_preview without waiting for the 5s TTL. ACL guarantee at compile time: UserPlotClient only — AgentPlotHandle has no editIntent (mx-bd4d67), so the agent-actor mistake is unreachable from this code path.

POST /plots/:id/status (src/server/handlers.ts changePlotStatusHandler, warren-e868 / pl-9d6a step 10) accepts {next: PlotStatus, dispatcher_handle?} and returns {summary: PlotSummary, event: PlotEvent} for optimistic UI. Handler-edge validation: PLOT_STATUSES typo guard rejects unknown next with 400; SPEC §6.5 transition matrix is enforced inside defaultPlotStatusChanger (reads current status, then assertStatusTransitionAllowed before setStatus) so warren never constructs invalid transitions. Resolver-then-hasPlot stack mirrors mx-cdf40d / mx-0ef603. NOT fire-and-log. Aggregator cache invalidated post-write. PlotIllegalStatusTransitionError → 409 / plot_illegal_status_transition.

STATUS_TRANSITIONS (src/plots/status-changer.ts, warren-e868 / pl-9d6a step 10) is the SPEC §6.5 whitelist: drafting→{ready,archived}, ready→{active,archived}, active→{done,archived}, done→{archived}, archived→{} (terminal). No self-loops, no back-edges. assertStatusTransitionAllowed(plotId, current, next) throws PlotIllegalStatusTransitionError. defaultPlotStatusChanger.change opens UserPlotClient, reads current status to re-check under fresh on-disk state, calls setStatus, then snapshots Plot+events and returns the per-project ChangePlotStatusResult (CreatePlotResult shape + the matching status_changed event found via tail-first walk for data.to===current.status).

PlotIllegalStatusTransitionError (src/plots/errors.ts, code=plot_illegal_status_transition, warren-e868) maps to 409 in src/server/errors.ts (state-transition shape, same envelope as PlotIntentFrozenError). Raised at the handler edge via assertStatusTransitionAllowed (defense in depth) and inside defaultPlotStatusChanger before setStatus — both surface to the wire as 409.

POST /plots/:id/questions/:event_id/answer (src/server/handlers.ts answerPlotQuestionHandler, warren-e1ac / pl-9d6a step 12) is the question-answer surface; :event_id is the ISO 'at' timestamp of the targeted question_posed event (PlotEvent has no synthetic id). Same handler-shape as intent/status/attach: resolve plot via plotResolver → defensive hasPlot re-check (ProjectLacksPlotError, 400) → delegate to deps.plotQuestionAnswerer (default defaultPlotQuestionAnswerer) → invalidate aggregator cache. Surfaces synchronously (NOT fire-and-log; user is waiting). Returns {event: PlotEvent} for optimistic UI splice.

Plot UI write handlers (GET/POST /plots, GET /plots/:id, POST /plots/:id/{intent,status,attachments,questions/:event_id/answer}, DELETE /plots/:id/attachments/:ref) all share a common shape: hasPlot gate → PlotResolver → resolveDispatcherHandle (operator fallback, mx-6a9788) → UserPlotClient (HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES narrows agent:* unreachable at compile time, mx-bd4d67). Unlike §11.O single-run dispatch which is fire-and-log on Plot-write failures, these write paths surface failures synchronously as typed 4xx — the user is waiting on the response. Handler-edge invariants enforced beyond the facade: status transitions are double-validated against SPEC §6.5 whitelist; intent edits reject at status done/archived; question-answer rejects already-answered questions (the Plot library does not guarantee one-answer-per-question across racing writers).

warren's SeedsCli SpawnFn (src/projects/clone.ts SpawnFn shape) has no stdin channel — only cwd + timeoutMs. When a sd subcommand only accepts input via '--plan -' / stdin (e.g. 'sd plan submit --plan -'), warren must write the payload to a tmp file via mkdtemp+writeFile under os.tmpdir() and pass --plan <file>, then unlink in a finally. Pattern landed in src/plot-plan-runs/synthesizer.ts submitSynthesizedPlan() for warren-99b2 / SPEC §11.Q. Future sd integrations that need stdin input must follow the same tmp-file workaround unless SpawnFn grows a stdin field.

GET /projects/:id/seeds/:seedId surfaces single-seed status read (warren-4015) using showSeed; gate order mirrors POST /plan-runs: project 404 → hasSeeds gate (ProjectLacksSeedsError, 400) → seedsCli configured gate (ValidationError, 400) → showSeed call. Response shape is narrow {id, status, blockedBy} — only what PlotDetail BatchDispatch needs to drop closed seeds before fanning out POST /runs.

POST /runs accepts mode='batch'|'interactive' (warren-b3b9 / pl-0344 step 4). When mode='interactive' the handler in src/server/handlers.ts: (1) requires plotId (400 validation_error if absent), (2) uses interactiveAgent body field as agent name when set (overrides 'agent'), (3) loads Plot envelope via defaultPlotContextReader (best-effort, historyTail=0 since no prior turn events exist) and wraps the operator prompt in buildInteractivePrompt's <plot_context>+<user_message> envelope so turn 1 and turn N share the same shape, (4) appends a user_message event onto the new run after spawnRun returns. Mode validation is parseRunMode (defaults to 'batch'). The plot_id format/existence check (assertPlotIdDispatchable) still runs first so the more-specific error wins. POST /runs/:id/messages is the follow-up surface: thin envelope over spawnInteractiveTurn, returns 202 with {run, burrow, userMessageEvent, priorRunId, plotContextDegraded}.

bootPauseDetector wiring in src/server/main.ts (warren-2976) follows the bootPlanRunCoordinator boot pattern (mx-985743): builder takes the ServerDeps bag, returns a handle whose stop() goes on bootServer's teardown chain BEFORE bridges/burrow/db tear down so an in-flight tick drains. Default-disabled — WARREN_PAUSE_DETECTOR_ENABLED=1 turns it on; WARREN_PAUSE_DETECTOR_TICK_MS (default 15_000) sets cadence. The respawn seam is wired to a logging-only no-op pending warren-3de8/d22e — the interactive run primitive (warren-1117) is the consumer that will fold answer/timeout context into a new burrow turn.

POST /brainstorm (src/server/handlers.ts createBrainstormHandler, warren-d22e / pl-0344 step 8) is the fresh-start UX wrapper: atomically creates a draft Plot via defaultPlotCreator (status=drafting, empty intent, name defaults to 'Untitled brainstorm') and dispatches the first interactive turn against the built-in 'brainstorm' agent bound to that Plot. Body: {project_id, prompt, name?, agent?, dispatcher_handle?, providerOverride?, modelOverride?, ref?}. The underlying primitives (POST /plots + POST /runs mode=interactive) remain separately reachable; this endpoint exists so the UI doesn't sequence them with partial-failure rollback. Reuses the existing buildInteractivePrompt + spawnRun + appendUserMessage pipeline from createRunHandler's interactive branch \u2014 no new sandbox shape. /brainstorm is added to API_PREFIXES (handlers.ts) AND the duplicate WARREN_API_PATH_PREFIXES (src/preview/proxy.ts) \u2014 the warren-api-prefixes-stay-in-sync test pins this.

GET /plots/needs-attention/count (src/server/handlers.ts, warren-d693 / pl-0344 step 9) MUST be registered in ROUTE_TABLE before /plots/:id so the matchRoute first-match-wins router doesn't capture 'needs-attention' as an :id param — same ordering rule that puts /plots/:id/formalize before /plots/:id. listPlotsHandler also accepts ?filter=needs_attention (validates against a singleton whitelist for forward compat) and composes with ?status= by post-filtering the listNeedsAttention rows client-side.

GET /runs envelope (src/server/handlers.ts listRunsHandler, warren-ee50 / pl-b0c0 step 1) is now {runs, total, costTotalUsd, costPricedCount, limit, offset}. Parses ?limit (default 100, must be 1..500) and ?offset (default 0, non-negative) via parseRunsPagination(); rejects out-of-band with ValidationError. Aggregate fields describe the FULL filtered set (predicate=?project|?agent), not just the returned window \u2014 so the Runs page header can show all-time totals (acceptance criterion 2 of pl-b0c0) while the table paginates. ListRunsResponse mirror lives in src/ui/src/api/types.ts; runsApi.list (src/ui/src/api/client.ts) accepts limit+offset in ListRunsFilter.

POST /plots/:id/rename (src/server/handlers.ts renamePlotHandler, src/plots/renamer.ts, warren-bed0 / pl-b0c0 step 3) is the wire entry for the PlotDetail header rename affordance. Mirrors the editPlotIntentHandler pipeline: parse {name, dispatcher_handle?} (trim + non-empty check + unknown-field reject at the handler edge), resolveDispatcherHandle, plotResolver.resolve (404 on null), defensive hasPlot re-check (ProjectLacksPlotError 400), delegate to deps.plotRenamer (defaultPlotRenamer) which opens a UserPlotClient and calls UserPlotClient.rename. Returns full PlotEnvelope; invalidates aggregator cache so a follow-up GET /plots sees the new name without the 5s TTL. Renames are NOT frozen-at-done — the name is pure metadata; the SPEC §6 freeze applies only to the intent body. NOT fire-and-log (user is waiting on the click). Route registered after /plots/:id/intent in src/server/handlers.ts ROUTES.

GET /analytics/cost (src/server/handlers.ts listCostAnalyticsHandler, warren-cf63 / pl-b0c0 step 6) returns a single payload with totals + all eight breakdowns (date/project/plan/plot/run/agent/model/provider). Query params: from/to (ISO8601 datetime, validated via new Date()), projectId. Defaults to last 30 days when neither bound is set. Handler pipeline: RunsRepo.listForAnalytics({projectId,from,to}) -> hydrateRunsUsage -> PlanRunsRepo.resolvePlanForRunIds -> extractProviderModel (reads renderedAgentJson.frontmatter via readProviderFrontmatter) -> buildCostAnalytics. Added '/analytics' to API_PREFIXES AND src/preview/proxy.ts WARREN_API_PATH_PREFIXES (warren-63e1 parity test enforces this).

src/server/main.ts (warren-8d3d / pl-9088 step 10) is split into src/server/main/ subdirectory: utils.ts (defaultSpawn + closeDatabase + redactDbUrl + resolvePgPoolMax + parseTrueEnv + parseIntEnv), logging.ts (6 pino→narrow logger adapters: bridge/probe/scheduler/planRun/pause/previewEviction), preview-wiring.ts (createPreviewAuthAndProxy — R-19/SPEC §11.L token × mode × host matrix), deps.ts (buildServerDeps — ServerDeps literal + plotAggregator), index.ts (bootServer orchestrator + CLI entry + public type re-exports, 472 lines). Test files split 1:1 alongside (utils.test.ts/logging.test.ts/preview-wiring.test.ts + the original resolvePgPoolMax suite at main/index.test.ts) so the extracted helpers regain direct coverage — otherwise the split would have dropped lines coverage below the 90% floor since boot-only code lives in the 0%-executed bootServer body. Importers (src/cli/commands/serve.{ts,test.ts}, src/server/index.ts) re-point to main/index.ts. scripts/file-size-budgets.json entry for main.ts removed; biome.json noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity override updated main.ts -> main/index.ts (bootServer body still trips the threshold). The CLI entry shebang (import.meta.main block) lives in main/index.ts; bun run src/server/main/index.ts replaces the prior src/server/main.ts invocation in the Phase-12 supervisor exec path.
plots/ index.ts is a composing route table for plots subdirectory, re-exporting the split handlers list, sync, workbench and remaining monolithic plots.ts handlers.

Split src/server/handlers/runs.ts into runs/ subdirectory domain modules (dispatch, lifecycle, events, preview, pause-resume) and runs.test.ts into modular test files (runs.dispatch.test, runs.interactive.test, runs.events.test) with shared test helpers (warren-6566)

Split src/server/handlers/plan-runs.test.ts (1318 lines) into plan-runs.test-helpers.ts and five focused test files under 500 lines: create, plot, get, cancel, events. Removed old monolithic test file and updated file-size budget.

Split src/server/handlers/runs.preview.test.ts into three focused files (runs.preview-launch.test.ts, runs.preview-proxy.test.ts, runs.preview-eviction.test.ts) and a shared runs.preview-test-helpers.ts helper, removing the old monolithic file and lowering its budget.
Split projects.test.ts into five focused files under server/handlers to keep per-file line count under the budget

name: post-runs-idempotency-window
description: POST /runs idempotency (warren-d525): createRunHandler (src/server/handlers/runs/dispatch.ts) reads the Idempotency-Key header and routes the spawn through deps.idempotencyStore.run(projectId, key, dispatch) (src/server/idempotency.ts, in-memory TTL window, default 10min). The dispatch closure wraps spawnRun + interactive appendUserMessage + bridges.start so ALL side effects only fire on a cache miss; a hit replays the cached 201 {run, burrow} body. Cached value is the in-flight promise so concurrent duplicates dedupe; a rejected dispatch evicts the entry so retries re-spawn. Absent header preserves always-spawn. Keyed by (projectId, key). Wired as a default in buildServerDeps (src/server/main/deps.ts).
